danger_signals.visual_form: cookies accumulating from top and right edges of playing field
danger_signals.meaning: impending game over state; world-state of failure approaching

danger_signals.visual_form: playing field population density increasing as new cookies enter
danger_signals.meaning: player losing control of the puzzle space; danger escalates with level progression

safety_signals.visual_form: same-colored cookies aligned in complete rows or columns visually distinct
safety_signals.meaning: player has agency and can clear lines; world-state is manageable
player has tool to force matches of any type; safety valve mechanic

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Yoshi branding and character licensing in title screen and UI; Yoshi serves as thematic anchor for puzzle aesthetic

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mario characters in unused versus mode graphics (Mario, Bowser, Peach)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bowser depicted with surrender flag in unused cutscene; Peach shown as angry in early sprite variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nintendo licensing; character appearances in puzzle mode narrative (unused but present in VRAM)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: grid size increases progressively; cookie spawn rate escalates; visual complexity compounds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player rotation mechanics remain constant but puzzle space becomes more constrained; cognitive difficulty rises

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: predefined static grid presented; limited number of moves displayed as constraint
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player forced to plan ahead; no continuous spawning pressure; tactical vs. reactive gameplay mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: split-screen layout showing two separate grids side-by-side; opposing player's grid visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player actions directly disrupt opponent grid; competitive pressure element manifests through visual parity and shared screen real estate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: grid overflow; cookies exceed playfield boundaries; trophy/sad thought bubble graphics (unused but designed)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: loss state signaled through sprite expressions (sad thought bubble for loser); winner receives trophy visual

environmental_storytelling.example: round endings show Yoshi in VRAM; unused cutscenes with Mario obscured by foreground plane suggest narrative progression tied to puzzle rounds
BPS logo variants (ornate lion vs. blocky) and copyright screen differences establish regional market positioning; musical stings differ to reinforce regional identity
NES/Game Boy versions used cookie jar skit in intro; SNES version abandoned this; visual design language shifted between platforms to match hardware capabilities and market expectations
Yoshi-head cookie as wildcard wild-card piece integrates character brand identity directly into puzzle mechanic; visual symbol = game affordance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: pieces clear/disappear from grid; visual feedback confirms player action success
cookies enter from top and right edges at accelerating rate; visual manifestation of escalating pressure
rotates individual lines (Rubik's Cube-style); visual indicator of player agency on grid
visible on split-screen competing for same objective; behavioral parity with player creates tension through visual mirroring

notes: Yoshi's Cookie is a minimalist puzzle game with limited world-state communication beyond grid saturation and match mechanics. Danger is purely systemic (overflow) rather than narrative/faction-based. The game contains unused character sprite assets suggesting planned versus-mode storytelling (Mario, Bowser, Peach victory/defeat states) that never manifested in final build. Regional visual differences (logo, copyright, intro animations) signal market-specific branding rather than world-state variation. No true faction conflict or political narrative present; game is mechanics-driven aesthetic-light puzzle experience. Wildcard cookie serves as primary non-grid visual communication of player advantage.
